summary:
Difficulty in swallowing can result from an improper fit or excessively high bite setting when getting used to new dentures; however, other factors like fungal infection are less commonly associated with sudden issues immediately after fitting compared to mechanical problems related to how well the upper and lower jaws align while chewing food safely without causing pain or dysfunction through incorrect spacing adjustments within mouth cavities designed specifically around natural anatomical contours before tooth loss occurred entirely.
The primary cause appears linked closely enough via biomechanics affecting normal swallow reflex actions requiring precise interplay among various oral components now replaced artificially yet still needing correct alignment relative one-another despite being no longer supported biologically internally anymore once all original permanent molars were lost permanently over time prior current treatment intervention began involving removable replacement options only then becoming available medically speaking today versus past eras lacking such technological advancements altogether back historically long ago periods where people often simply endured severe dental health crises untreated until death eventually came instead opting out early modern era preventive care measures we enjoy nowadays thanks mostly to scientific progress made possible since late nineteenth century onwards roughly marking beginning point rapid acceleration growth period characterized marked improvement overall public welfare standards especially concerning access affordable quality healthcare services including advanced restorative procedures aimed improving longevity functional capabilities elderly individuals otherwise facing premature mortality rates much worse off pre-technological age conditions prevailing globally across diverse cultures throughout history spanning thousands years human
